dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need someone with no ties to this mess. Someone who'll look at the evidence instead of the gossip.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A shipment of {quest_item} vanished from the storehouse in {location} two nights ago. {target_npc} was the last person seen near the building, so naturally everyone has decided they're guilty. But I know {target_npc}, and they wouldn't steal a crumb from a dead man's plate. The real thief left tracks heading {location_hint}, but nobody cares because they've already made up their minds. If I don't find proof of who actually took the goods before the council meets, {target_npc} gets banished.
dialogueChunks.outro: Suspicion is a poison, and this settlement is drinking deep. Help me find the truth before an innocent person pays for someone else's crime.

playerResponses.text: If there are tracks, there's a trail. I'll find the real thief.
Clearing someone's name while the settlement wants blood? What's in it for me?
Local disputes aren't my concern. Work it out yourselves.

dialogueChunks.intro: People are whispering, {player_name}, and the whispers are getting louder. I need help before they turn into shouts.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} has been leaving {location} at odd hours and meeting with travelers nobody recognizes. The settlement thinks it's conspiracy: selling information to the {enemy_type}, plotting against the council, smuggling contraband. The truth is quieter than that. {target_npc} has been bartering for {quest_item} to surprise the settlement with repairs to the old watchtower, but the sellers are from a rival settlement and the meetings had to be secret. I need you to find the traders {location_hint} and bring back proof of what was actually being exchanged.
dialogueChunks.outro: One misunderstood kindness is about to destroy a good person's reputation. We don't have much time.

playerResponses.text: Secret good deeds mistaken for treachery. I'll set the record straight.
Tracking down secretive traders in dangerous territory? Not cheap work.
If {target_npc} wanted to avoid suspicion, they should have been transparent.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, half this settlement thinks the other half is cheating them, and it's about to boil over.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The rationing system in {location} has people convinced that {target_npc} is giving extra portions to favorites. I've seen the ledgers, and every distribution is fair to the grain. But perception beats reality when bellies are growling. The real shortage is because {enemy_type} have been raiding the supply wagons {location_hint}, and nobody's tracked them down yet. If I can recover {quest_item} from the raided wagons and show that the missing food went to monsters, not neighbors, maybe people will stop blaming each other.
dialogueChunks.outro: Hunger makes people suspicious. Proof that nobody is cheating will calm things down faster than any speech.

playerResponses.text: Recovering stolen supplies and clearing names at once? I'll handle it.
Chasing raiders through hostile territory for a community's peace of mind. What do I get?
Ration disputes are above my skill set. Good luck.
